instruction: Using the provided first aid reference text, generate a realistic first-person account of a layperson performing first aid — with 2–3 deliberate errors embedded — followed by identification of each error, why it is dangerous, and the corrected procedure. Errors must be real mistakes people commonly make, not invented ones. Ground the corrected procedure primarily in the reference text. You may supplement with widely accepted first aid facts ONLY if the reference text partially covers the topic — but clearly flag the supplementation.
constraints: Errors must be grounded in contrast with the reference text — do not invent dangers not implied by the text. The flawed procedure must read naturally, as if a real person wrote it. The corrected procedure must primarily use steps from the reference text. Do not add procedures, dosages, or medical guidance beyond the seed text unless supplementation is necessary because the reference text only partially covers the topic. Any supplemented information must be explicitly flagged in the output. Rate each error as risky or critical. If the seed text has no actionable first aid procedure, output skip=true.
